summary: End-to-end testing of JWT auth, demo seed, landing/login pages, super-admin impersonation, and dashboard role routing. Backend: 22/22 pytest cases pass (login success/failure, /auth/me, refresh, logout, demo-accounts public, impersonation, RBAC on /auth/users, 5-attempt lockout). Frontend: landing page renders all 13 expected data-testid sections with the correct hero copy 'Orchestrate national distribution with intelligence built in'; login split-screen + demo-account autofill works; super_admin -> Super Admin Console; impersonation -> retailer dashboard with 'Return to admin' button; returning restores admin session; manufacturer/distributor/retailer logins all land on /dashboard with correct entity (Unilever, SUARA & CO.); root redirect (logged-in users visiting / go to /dashboard) works; unauth /dashboard redirects to /login; bad credentials show error.

minor.issue: Doesn't invalidate the access token server-side. Token continues to work after /auth/logout because logout only clears cookies; Bearer tokens remain valid until natural expiry (30 min). Consider a token-blacklist or short access TTL for stricter logout semantics. Not a blocker — frontend clears localStorage on its own.

action_items: Optional: add a server-side blacklist for revoked access tokens on logout (low priority).
Pytest marker pytest.mark.order('last') emits a warning — install pytest-order or remove the marker; not functionally broken.
critical_code_review_comments: Auth module is well-structured: bcrypt for hashing, JWT HS256, public_user() strips sensitive fields, register_login_failure handles lockout correctly with 5-attempt threshold and auto-clear after expiry.
/auth/refresh accepts token from body OR cookie -- good fallback strategy.
/auth/demo-accounts is public and never leaks password hashes or invitation tokens -- verified.

Impersonation endpoint stamps 'impersonated_by' in the JWT payload, allowing future server-side audit; current implementation only returns it in the response body (the encoded JWT keeps standard claims). Consider adding it to the access token claims if you want server-side detection.
JWT_SECRET is loaded from env and raises at startup if missing -- good fail-fast.
SessionContext + api.js Bearer interceptor + 401 -> /login?expired=1 redirect is clean and matches the playbook.
Layout shows 'stop-impersonating-btn' only when impersonating -- works correctly in tests.
LoginPage demo-account click autofills email + password -- usability is excellent for a POC demo.
test_credentials: All demo accounts use password 'TradeKonekt2026!'. Admin: admin@tradekonekt.io. Manufacturer: unilever@tradekonekt.io. Distributors: lagos./abuja./ph.distributor@tradekonekt.io. Retailers: retailer1..5@tradekonekt.io. Test file resets retailer4 lockout via a post-test DB unset (already cleared in this iteration).
seed_data_creation: No new seed created. Existing seed_demo_users.py is idempotent and already produced the 10 demo accounts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth layer is solid. test_auth.py covers 22 cases (login, /me, refresh, logout, impersonation, RBAC, lockout). Lockout test uses retailer4 and resets via a successful login after the test (no explicit DB cleanup needed because the lockout test ends with a 429, but if you re-run, the retailer4 account may still be locked for up to 15 min). To unlock manually: db.users.update_one({'email':'retailer4@tradekonekt.io'},{'$unset':{'lockout_until':'','failed_attempts':''}}). When testing frontend flows that involve impersonation -> return-to-admin, add a 2-3s wait after click because the flow re-fetches /auth/me before re-rendering.
rca of the issue: Initial flaky failures in run 1 (manufacturer-login timeout after switch-account, root-redirect not happening, super-admin-console not visible after return) were all order/timing artefacts in the same Playwright session -- when the same flows were re-run in isolation with fresh cookies + localStorage cleared, every one passed. No actual product bug. Recommend longer post-click waits (>=2.5s) in any future automated tests that exercise the impersonation -> return loop.
